content.summary: The paper proposes adaptive certificates for stochastic optimization under covariate shift. The main result combines an anytime concentration bound with a restart mechanism whose threshold depends on an estimated density ratio.
content.strengthsAndWeaknesses: Strengths: the problem is important, the certificate is operational, and the evaluation covers both synthetic and image-classification shifts. Weaknesses: the original theorem statement hid a bounded-ratio assumption, and the first ablation did not isolate the restart rule.
questions: Can the authors state explicitly where the bounded density-ratio assumption enters Proposition 2?
How much of the gain remains when the restart rule is disabled but the confidence sequence is retained?
content.limitations: The guarantee does not cover unbounded density ratios, and the experiments do not test adversarial shifts.

content.title: Author rebuttal
content.text: We agree that the bounded density-ratio assumption should have been explicit. We added it to Proposition 2 and supplied a line-by-line proof patch. We also ran the requested no-restart ablation: the confidence sequence alone improves shifted accuracy by 0.8 points, while the full method improves it by 2.7 points. The attached table reports all seeds and intervals.

content.title: Reviewer follow-up
content.text: The revised statement resolves my main soundness concern. The validator artifact independently confirms the corrected constant. The no-restart ablation is also informative; please include confidence intervals in the final paper.

scoreChanges.reason: The theorem assumption is now explicit and the requested ablation supports the mechanism claim.

content.title: Author final follow-up
content.text: Confirmed. The camera-ready checklist now requires confidence intervals for every shifted-accuracy table, and the corrected Proposition 2 wording is included verbatim in the revision artifact.

content.title: Reviewer final justification
content.text: After rebuttal, I raise the recommendation from 4 to 5. The revised Proposition 2 states the bounded-ratio assumption, the proof artifact checks the corrected constant, and the new ablation supports the claimed contribution of the restart rule. The author final follow-up also commits to retaining confidence intervals.

content.summary: This is a careful and useful treatment of certification under covariate shift, with a strong connection between the theoretical stopping rule and the implemented algorithm.
content.strengthsAndWeaknesses: The empirical protocol is unusually transparent and the result is novel. The main limitation is reliance on density-ratio estimation, which may be unstable in very high dimensions.
questions: Could the authors report sensitivity to density-ratio clipping?
content.limitations: The evaluation covers moderate but not extreme shifts.

content.title: Meta-review
content.text: The reviewers agree that the paper is technically strong and original. The only material soundness concern was resolved by an explicit assumption, a checked proof patch, and a targeted ablation. I recommend acceptance and nominate the paper for spotlight consideration.

content.title: Decision: Accept (Spotlight)
content.text: The paper is accepted to ICML 2026 as a spotlight presentation. The final version must retain the clarified assumption and full ablation intervals.
